data.title: Strategy Stock Soars Today: What's Happening?
data.description:
• Strategy stock is among today’s top performers. What’s behind MSTR gains?

A Deepening Bitcoin Supply Squeeze

On‑chain data from CryptoQuant shows that Bitcoin reserves on exchanges have been falling steadily since 2023–2024, a structural shift that reduces the amount of Bitcoin available for trading.

Asset managers such as BlackRock, through the iShares Bitcoin Trust, along with Morgan Stanley, Charles Schwab and Goldman Sachs, have all expanded their Bitcoin exposure through ETFs and brokerage channels. At the same time, Strategy continues to accumulate aggressively and now reportedly controls close to 4% of Bitcoin's total supply.

That level of ownership makes Strategy behave like a leveraged bet on Bitcoin scarcity.

Exchange Flows Confirm The Trend

CryptoQuant analyst Axel Adler Jr. highlighted a sharp reversal in exchange flows during March 2026. A 30‑day net inflow of roughly +94,000 BTC flipped into a peak outflow near -300,000 BTC, signaling rapid withdrawal activity.

By April 21, outflows had moderated to around -98,000 BTC, but flows remained negative overall.

Over seven weeks, total exchange reserves dropped by more than 105,000 BTC, falling from about 2.79 million to 2.68 million. Even during price dips into the $67,000–$72,000 range, there was no meaningful uptick in Bitcoin being sent back to exchanges, suggesting holders were not interested in selling.

Strategy Technical Analysis

Strategy is sitting well off its February low and still far below its July 2025 peak, which frames the current move as a rebound inside a larger, choppier 12-month picture. The stock is trading 30.3% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 18.9% above its 100-day SMA, which suggests short-term and intermediate trend pressure is currently tilted to buyers.

The moving average structure is still mixed: the 20-day SMA is above the 50-day SMA (a bullish near-term alignment), but the death cross from October 2025 (50-day SMA below the 200-day SMA) keeps the longer-term trend filter cautious. That split often shows up when a stock is bouncing hard, but hasn't fully repaired the bigger downtrend.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is above its signal line with a positive histogram, which points to strengthening upside momentum in the current swing. In everyday terms, MACD being above the signal line means recent price action is improving faster than the prior baseline.

  • Key Support: $156.00 — an area where buyers have tended to show up on pullbacks.
  • Key Resistance: $190.00 — a level where rallies have recently struggled to push through.

MSTR Shares Are Rising

MSTR Price Action: Strategy shares were up 8.11% at $177.27 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Newsmax Stock Wednesday?
data.description:
Newsmax, Inc. (NYSE:NMAX) shares rose 40.80% on Wednesday. The primary catalyst for today’s rally appears to be a massive spike in call option volume.

Investors purchased 13,430 call options on Wednesday, signaling significant bullish sentiment or speculative “meme stock” interest.

This activity represents an approximately 1,505% increase over the stock’s typical daily volume of 837 call options.

Newsmax Technical Analysis

Newsmax is currently trading within a significant range, having experienced a 12-month decline of 55.88%. The stock is trading 63.7% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 58.4% above its 50-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. However, it remains 3.2% below its 200-day SMA, suggesting some long-term bearishness.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 66.33, indicating that the stock is nearing overbought territory, which could suggest a potential pullback. The moving average convergence divergence (MACD) is bullish, with the MACD line above the signal line, indicating positive momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $10.50 — This level may act as a barrier for upward movement.
  • Key Support: N/A — No defined support level currently identified.

Company Context

Newsmax is a multi-platform media company. The company provides original news and lifestyle updates from its linear cable television and over-the-top (OTT) news channels, websites, proprietary database, publishing products, and e-commerce products.

It uses original news and editorial content to draw large numbers of readers to its media outlets to sell advertising, print, and online information products. The company’s business operations are conducted through two operating segments: Broadcasting and Digital.

Maximum revenue is generated from the Broadcasting segment, which produces and licenses news, business news, and lifestyle content for distribution through cable television systems, direct broadcast satellite operators, and telecommunication companies, mainly in the United States.

Newsmax Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 14, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 3 cents (up from Loss of 49 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $49.46 million (up from $45.30 million)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Strong_buy rating with an average price target of $21.50. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Noble Capital Markets: Initiated with Outperform (Target $23.00) (Aug. 21, 2025)
  • Maxim Group: Initiated with Buy (Target $20.00) (July 24, 2025)

NMAX Stock Price Activity: Newsmax shares were up 40.80% at $10.25 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Bigc Studio via Shutterstock

data.title: Wayve Scores $8.6 Billion Valuation: NVIDIA, Microsoft Fuel British Robotaxi Invasion
data.description:
On Wednesday, Counterpoint Research outlined Wayve's rapid progress in autonomous driving, highlighting strong backing, key partnerships, and a critical phase ahead for commercialization.

Strong Funding and Strategic Partnerships Drive Expansion

In early 2026, Wayve unveiled a flurry of announcements that solidified its robotaxi and autonomy rollout plans.  The $1.2 billion Series D investment round announced in March gives it an $8.6 billion valuation and will fuel its global expansion.

The company is working closely with Uber for robotaxi pilots and with Nissan for both robotaxi deployments and consumer driver-assistance systems.

Scalable AI Approach Positions Wayve for Growth

Ali highlighted that Wayve's driving software uses a single, adaptable AI system that can operate in new cities with minimal adjustments, giving it an advantage in scaling robotaxi services.

The company plans to launch robotaxi trials in London in 2026, followed by Tokyo with Nissan, and expand to more than 10 cities through its partnership with Uber.

Critical 12–24 Months Will Test Commercial Viability

Ali emphasized that the next one to two years will be decisive as Wayve moves from testing to real-world deployment.

He said success in upcoming robotaxi pilots will determine whether the company can scale globally and prove its business model, especially as it competes with established players and navigates regulatory and safety challenges.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Salesforce Advances AI Strategy With Google Cloud Partnership
data.description:
Technical Analysis

The broader market saw gains, with the Technology sector up 1.65% today. Salesforce’s rise aligns with the sector’s performance, indicating a favorable environment for tech stocks.

Salesforce is currently trading within a 52-week range of $163.52 to $296.05, suggesting it is positioned toward the middle of this range. The stock is trading 5.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), indicating short-term strength, while it sits 11.4% below its 100-day SMA, suggesting some intermediate-term weakness.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Salesforce is slated to provide its next financial update on May 27, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: $2.79 (Up from $2.58)
  • Revenue Estimate: $11.06 billion (Up from $9.83 billion)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $265.79. Recent analyst moves include:

data.title: What's Going On With Philip Morris Stock Today?
data.description:
The firm flagged limited disruption from Middle East tensions and trimmed its full-year outlook, citing higher input and transport costs.

Quarterly Details

Middle East

The Stamford, Connecticut-based company said the Middle East conflict had a limited impact in the quarter under review, affecting shipments in travel retail and select regional markets.

It noted that higher energy prices and supply disruptions have not yet significantly altered consumer behavior.

The firm added that while uncertainty remains, its outlook does not assume prolonged disruption but factors in higher input and transport costs.

Outlook

Philip Morris projects second-quarter adjusted EPS of $2.02-$2.07, versus the $2.12 analyst estimate.

The firm lowered 2026 adjusted EPS guidance from $8.38-$8.53 to $8.36-$8.51, versus the $8.40 analyst estimate.

Philip Morris lowered 2026 GAAP EPS guidance from $7.87-$8.02 to $7.56-$7.71 vs $7.97 analyst estimate.

PM Price Action: Philip Morris shares are trading higher by 6.60% to $163.41 at last check Wednesday.

Photo via Shutterstock
